what_it_does: Curates a candidate set of Tim's real pending architectural decisions from project history, derives six decision-subtype kinds from their shapes, and hands the curated set and subtype vocabulary to a lead for authoring the first decision cards.
observations: Lists seven pending decisions, each tagged with a KIND such as identity, addressability/shape, taxonomy, capability/authorization, foundation, or boundary.
Maps DNA #1-19 overlaps onto the candidate set to avoid deciding the same question twice.
Defines a unified decision-card archetype parameterized by kind: {meaning, options[{label, implication, recommended}], state}.
Identifies the merge-SA save-back decision as the only live decision:// row and the natural first card.
Includes an explicit honesty caveat that no additional crisp pending decisions were found beyond this set and no filler was manufactured.
standouts: The six derived subtype kinds unify identity, addressability, taxonomy, capability, foundation, and boundary decisions under one reusable archetype.
The merge-SA decision is the only already-typed, live decision and is positioned as the shape template.
The document self-limits its scope, refusing to invent decisions when history surfaced none.
conventions: Uses uppercase section headers and structured markdown bullet lists.
References external identifiers such as UNION-DIVERGENCE-LEDGER §A, DNA #1-19, decision://global/merge-sa-authorize, and commit 3b5107a.
Tags each decision with a KIND label drawn from a derived vocabulary.
Follows an interface-first, lead-curates pattern where the AI surfaces candidates and the human finalizes the decision set.
concerns: Heavy reliance on external documents and identifiers without inline context makes the file opaque without corpus history.
Some decisions are conditional or residual, e.g., 'reaches Tim only if a residual survives,' leaving scope unclear.
The foundation decision is described as still being composed by the lead for Tim, so it is not fully authored here.
notes: A curation handoff document that turns scattered pending architectural questions into a typed decision-card backlog and a reusable subtype vocabulary.
questions: What is the exact content and status of the referenced UNION-DIVERGENCE-LEDGER §A and DNA #1-19 items?
What criteria will the lead use to finalize the first decision-set from these candidates?
How will the recall_for_decision context bundle be generated for each card?
What is the intended consumer of this file: a human lead, the interface, or both?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to surface real pending decisions and derive subtype kinds; the file does exactly that, plus maps DNA overlaps and hands off to a lead.
apparent_themes: Avoiding duplicate decisions across overlapping workstreams.
Honest scope and anti-filler epistemic discipline.
Lead-mediated authoring where AI surfaces and human curates.
intention_signals: It is trying to bootstrap a decision-card interface by first inventorying genuine unresolved architectural questions, then extracting a small vocabulary of decision shapes so that one rendering component can present every pending choice consistently.
novelty: The explicit derivation of decision-subtype kinds from real pending decisions rather than from a predefined ontology, and the insistence that the interface's first content must come from curated human decisions, not AI-generated filler.
contribution: A curated candidate decision-set and a derived decision-subtype vocabulary that can seed a unified decision-card archetype and component catalog.
summary_for_embedding: A markdown curation document listing seven genuine pending architectural decisions for Tim, mapping DNA overlaps, deriving six decision-subtype kinds, and handing the set to a lead to produce the first decision cards using a merge-SA-shaped archetype.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the interface's first real content and a reusable decision-subtype taxonomy by surfacing and typing Tim's actual unresolved design questions.
